Items.Title: 使用 Soroban Python SDK 部署和调用具有 Stellar 帐户授权的 Auth 合约 | Deploying and Invoking An Auth Contract with Stellar Account Authorization using Soroban Python SDK
Items.Description:
在我之前的帖子中，我向您展示了如何使用 Soroban Python SDK 部署包装的代币合约。 在这篇文章中，我们将使用 Soroban Python SDK 来调用具有 stellar 帐户授权的 auth 合约。

首先，创建一个 Futurenet 账户，该账户将用于部署 auth 合约并使用 Soroban Python SDK 调用合约。 在Stellar Laboratory创建帐户并确保为其提供资金。 我将使用的帐户：

 Public Key GATQFKA7BV7LYYE6UJ2LRL56W3BZRRSWTINA6TG2EM5CCDORFIERAKWV Secret Key SCHKLGAGY7QGWUKUFDGHEQMSCPFZNMLEHQE6Y6WPGYGHQIM23T3NJE3C

我们将要使用的文件：

部署合约

要部署合约，请打开soroban_deploy_contract.py文件并调整以下参数：

 secret = "SCHKLGAGY7QGWUKUFDGHEQMSCPFZNMLEHQE6Y6WPGYGHQIM23T3NJE3C" #Put your Account Secret Key here rpc_server_url = "https://horizon-futurenet.stellar.cash:443/soroban/rpc" network_passphrase = Network.FUTURENET_NETWORK_PASSPHRASE contract_file_path = "./soroban_auth_contract.wasm"

将值替换为您自己的帐户密钥。
这可以根据您正在与之交互的网络进行更改； 在这种情况下，我们将使用 Futurenet。
使用您的更改保存 soroban_deploy_contract.py 文件，然后使用以下命令在命令提示符下运行脚本：

 python `soroban_deploy_contract.py`

脚本运行完毕后，您将收到一个合约 ID。 请务必将其保存以备后用。

 contract id: af0aadafacc6bcb73b6ec9aeee03350ca0c6a3430e099d4b5dd371c6e07cc214

调用合约

要调用 auth 合约，请打开soroban_auth_with_stellar_account.py文件并调整以下参数：

 contract_id = "af0aadafacc6bcb73b6ec9aeee03350ca0c6a3430e099d4b5dd371c6e07cc214" tx_submitter_kp = Keypair.from_secret( "SAAPYAPTTRZMCUZFPG3G66V4ZMHTK4TWA6NS7U4F7Z3IMUD52EK4DDEV" ) # If tx_submitter_kp and op_invoker_kp use the same account, the submission will fail, a bug? op_invoker_kp = Keypair.from_secret( "SCHKLGAGY7QGWUKUFDGHEQMSCPFZNMLEHQE6Y6WPGYGHQIM23T3NJE3C" )

将值替换为您自己的帐户密钥。
将值替换为您之前从部署合同中收到的合同 ID。

使用您的更改保存soroban_auth_with_account文件。 注意：以某种方式对tx_submitter_kp和op_invoker_kp使用相同的密钥对导致交易无法提交，这可能是一个错误。 如果它是一个错误，这是预料之中的，因为 Soroban 仍在开发中。

在运行脚本之前，我想解释一下脚本：

 func_name = "increment" args = [Address(op_invoker_kp.public_key), Uint32(10)] invocation = AuthorizedInvocation( contract_id=contract_id, function_name=func_name, args=args, sub_invocations=[], )

从上面的脚本中，我们将要调用的合约函数是以 Stellar 账户密钥对作为参数的increment函数。

现在我们将使用此命令运行脚本：

 python soroban_auth_with_account.py

我们会得到结果：

 transaction result: <SCVal [type=1, u32=<Uint32 [uint32=10]>]>

再次运行脚本，我们将得到结果：

 transaction result: <SCVal [type=1, u32=<Uint32 [uint32=20]>]>

我们调用的increment函数按预期工作。

关闭

在这篇博文中，我们介绍了使用 Soroban Python SDK 部署和调用具有帐户授权的 Auth 合同的步骤。 从脚本中，您将了解基本的 Soroban 帐户授权是如何在 Soroban Python SDK 中实现的。



原文:

In my previous post, I showed you how to deploy a wrapped token contract using Soroban Python SDK. In this post, we'll use Soroban Python SDK to invoke an auth contract with stellar account authorization.

First, create a Futurenet account, this account will be used to deploy the auth contract and invoke the contract using Soroban Python SDK. Create account at Stellar Laboratory and make sure to fund them. The account that i will be using :

Public Key  GATQFKA7BV7LYYE6UJ2LRL56W3BZRRSWTINA6TG2EM5CCDORFIERAKWV
Secret Key  SCHKLGAGY7QGWUKUFDGHEQMSCPFZNMLEHQE6Y6WPGYGHQIM23T3NJE3C

The files that we're gonna use :

soroban_auth_contract.wasm - The Auth Contract
soroban_deploy_contract.py - Python Script to Deploy the contract
soroban_auth_with_stellar_account - Python Script to Invoke the contract

Deploying The Contract

To deploy a contract, open the soroban_deploy_contract.py file and adjust the following parameters:

secret = "SCHKLGAGY7QGWUKUFDGHEQMSCPFZNMLEHQE6Y6WPGYGHQIM23T3NJE3C" #Put your Account Secret Key here
rpc_server_url = "https://horizon-futurenet.stellar.cash:443/soroban/rpc"
network_passphrase = Network.FUTURENET_NETWORK_PASSPHRASE
contract_file_path = "./soroban_auth_contract.wasm"

secret: the Secret Key of the Account that we're gonna use to deploy the auth contract. Replace the value with your own account secret key.
rpc_server_url: the URL of the RPC server you'll use to interact with the Soroban Futurenet network.
network_passphrase: the passphrase you'll use to select the network. This can be changed depending on the network you're interacting with; in this case, we'll use Futurenet.
contract_file_path: the path to the auth contract file.

Save the soroban_deploy_contract.py file with your changes and then run the script in a command prompt using this command :

python `soroban_deploy_contract.py`

Once the script has finished running, you'll receive a contract ID. Be sure to save this for later.

contract id: af0aadafacc6bcb73b6ec9aeee03350ca0c6a3430e099d4b5dd371c6e07cc214

Invoking the Contract

To invoke the auth contract, open the soroban_auth_with_stellar_account.py file and adjust the following parameters:

contract_id = "af0aadafacc6bcb73b6ec9aeee03350ca0c6a3430e099d4b5dd371c6e07cc214"
tx_submitter_kp = Keypair.from_secret(
    "SAAPYAPTTRZMCUZFPG3G66V4ZMHTK4TWA6NS7U4F7Z3IMUD52EK4DDEV"
)
# If tx_submitter_kp and op_invoker_kp use the same account, the submission will fail, a bug?
op_invoker_kp = Keypair.from_secret(
    "SCHKLGAGY7QGWUKUFDGHEQMSCPFZNMLEHQE6Y6WPGYGHQIM23T3NJE3C"
)

tx_submitter_kp: the secret key of the account you'll use to submit the transaction.
op_invoker_kp: the secret key of the account you'll use to invoke the contract function. Replace the value with your own account secret key.
contract_id: the contract ID of the auth contract you want to invoke. Replace the value with the contract ID you received from deploying contract before.

Save the soroban_auth_with_account file with your changes. Note : somehow using the same keypair for tx_submitter_kp and op_invoker_kp makes the transaction failed to be sumbited, maybe this is a bug. If it is a bug, this is expected since Soroban is still in development.

Before running the script, i want to explain the script :

func_name = "increment"
args = [Address(op_invoker_kp.public_key), Uint32(10)]

invocation = AuthorizedInvocation(
    contract_id=contract_id,
    function_name=func_name,
    args=args,
    sub_invocations=[],
)

From the script above the function of the contract that we're going to invoke is increment function with Stellar Account keypair as the argument.

Now we will run the script using this command :

python soroban_auth_with_account.py

We will get result:

transaction result: <SCVal [type=1, u32=<Uint32 [uint32=10]>]>

run the script once more, we will get result :

transaction result: <SCVal [type=1, u32=<Uint32 [uint32=20]>]>

The increment function that we called worked as intended.

Closing

In this blog post, we have gone over the steps to Deploy and Invoke Auth Contract with Account Authorization using Soroban Python SDK. From the script you will get an insight how basic Soroban Account Authorization implemented in Soroban Python SDK.
Items.Author: darkvallen

Items.Title: 了解 Java 中的面向对象编程 (OOP)：训练营毕业生指南 | Understanding Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) in Java: A guide for bootcamp graduates
Items.Description:
训练营可能是启动您的编程生涯的好方法，但它们可能并不总是提供对 Java 中面向对象编程 (OOP) 概念的全面理解。 作为一名训练营毕业生，我直接知道用额外的学习资源来补充你的教育对于成为一名成功的软件开发人员是多么重要。

我的背景是数字营销，在那里我作为客户和技术团队之间的联络人工作了 15 年多。 虽然我喜欢实施跟踪像素和解决标签管理问题，但我决定将自己的技能提升到一个新的水平并学习如何自己创建程序。 在 35 岁那年，我参加了一个线下训练营，该训练营应该涵盖 Java 编程的基础知识和中级水平。

然而，我很快意识到我从训练营获得的知识只适合初学者。 可以从 Udemy 和其他平台上的在线课程中获得相同数量的知识。 当我找到第一份开发人员工作时——不是 Java，而是 PHP——我意识到要在该领域取得成功真正重要的是理解 OOP 原则和在团队中有效工作的能力。

那么，OOP的原理是什么？ 在 OOP 中，一切都表示为一个对象，该对象具有属性和方法。 封装：这个原则是关于将相关的数据和函数分组到一个称为类的单元中。 封装有助于向其他类隐藏一个类的实现细节，这使得以后修改代码更容易，而不会影响程序的其他部分。

  • 继承：该原则允许您通过继承现有类的属性和方法来创建新类。 继承对于创建与现有类相似但具有一些附加或修改功能的新类很有用。

  • 多态性：该原则指的是对象具有多种形式的能力。 多态性允许您使用单个接口来表示多种类型的对象。

  • 抽象：该原则是关于关注对象的本质特征并忽略非本质特征。 抽象允许您通过隐藏底层实现的复杂性来创建更简单和更易于管理的代码。

    • 在尝试申请职位之前，您还应该了解一些基本规则。 它们具有优雅的缩写，可帮助您记住它们：KISS、DRY 和 SOLID。

    这五个原则被认为是构建健壮、可维护软件的基础。

    • 单一职责原则：该原则指出一个类应该只有一个改变的理由。 每个班级要做一件事，并且做好。

    • 开闭原则：该原则指出类应该对扩展开放，但对修改关闭。 换句话说，您应该能够添加新功能而无需更改现有代码。

    • 换句话说，您应该能够在任何可以使用其父类的地方使用子类。

    • 接口隔离原则：该原则指出，不应强迫客户端依赖于它们不使用的接口。 接口应针对特定用例进行定制。

    • 依赖倒置原则：该原则指出高层模块不应依赖于低层模块。 相反，它们都应该依赖于抽象。

    该原则指出软件应该设计得尽可能简单，没有不必要的复杂性。 简单的代码更易于阅读、测试和维护。

    我不会重复自己，这是不言自明的。 这适用于您为整个应用程序创建的一个类/模块。 尤其是当您使用框架时。

    框架......框架也是廉价训练营大多忽略的东西。 基本上，你花 1-2000 美元参加的整个训练营应该是介绍如何处理框架并了解你想设计应用程序的哪一部分：后端、前端，还是你想做整个从一开始的东西。

    除了 OOP 原则和框架之外，开发人员了解如何在团队中有效工作也很重要。 这包括文档编制、任务估计和版本控制等技能。 团队协作的一些基本工具包括：

    • 文档：良好的文档对于确保其他团队成员能够理解您的代码并有效地使用它至关重要。 这包括代码中的注释、README 文件和技术规范。

    • 任务估算：能够估算任务所需的时间对于有效的项目规划至关重要。 这涉及将任务分解为更小的子任务，估计每个子任务所需的时间，并跟踪进度。

    • 版本控制：Git 等版本控制工具允许团队成员在代码库上进行协作，而不会覆盖彼此的工作。 版本控制还使开发人员能够在必要时恢复到代码的早期版本。

    最后，值得注意的是，在 JetBrains 的 Hyperskill 等平台上进行一年的编码是加深对所选语言的理解并为您作为开发人员的第一份工作做准备的好方法。 虽然训练营可以提供一个很好的起点，但重要的是要继续自己学习和实践以培养自己的技能并成为一名成功的软件开发人员。



    原文:

    Bootcamps can be a great way to kickstart your programming career, but they may not always provide a comprehensive understanding of Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) concepts in Java. As a bootcamp graduate myself, I know firsthand how important it is to supplement your education with additional learning resources to succeed as a software developer.

    My background is in digital marketing, where I spent more than 15 years working as a liaison between clients and tech teams. While I enjoyed implementing tracking pixels and solving problems with tag management, I decided that I wanted to take my skills to the next level and learn how to create programs myself. At the age of 35, I enrolled in an offline bootcamp that was supposed to cover the basics to intermediate levels of programming in Java.

    However, I soon realized that the knowledge I gained from the bootcamp was only suitable for beginners. The same amount of knowledge could be obtained from online courses on Udemy and other platforms. When I landed my first job as a developer - not in Java, but in PHP - I realized that what was really important for success in the field was an understanding of OOP principles and the ability to work effectively in a team.

    So, what are the principles of OOP? In OOP, everything is represented as an object, which has properties and methods. The four main principles of OOP are:

    1. Encapsulation: This principle is about grouping related data and functions together into a single unit called a class. Encapsulation helps to hide the implementation details of a class from other classes, which makes it easier to modify the code in the future without affecting the other parts of the program.

    2. Inheritance: This principle allows you to create a new class by inheriting the properties and methods of an existing class. Inheritance is useful for creating new classes that are similar to existing ones, but with some additional or modified functionality.

    3. Polymorphism: This principle refers to the ability of objects to take on multiple forms. Polymorphism allows you to use a single interface to represent multiple types of objects.

    4. Abstraction: This principle is about focusing on the essential features of an object and ignoring the non-essential ones. Abstraction allows you to create simpler and more manageable code by hiding the complexity of the underlying implementation.

    There are also some essential sets of rules that you should know before you even try to apply for a position. They have elegant abbreviations that help you memorize them: KISS, DRY and SOLID.

    SOLID: SOLID is an acronym that stands for Single Responsibility, Open-Closed, Liskov Substitution, Interface Segregation, and Dependency Inversion. These five principles are considered fundamental to building robust, maintainable software.

    • Single Responsibility Principle: This principle states that a class should have only one reason to change. Each class should do one thing, and do it well.

    • Open-Closed Principle: This principle states that classes should be open for extension, but closed for modification. In other words, you should be able to add new functionality without having to change existing code.

    • Liskov Substitution Principle: This principle states that subclasses should be substitutable for their base classes. In other words, you should be able to use a subclass wherever you can use its parent class.

    • Interface Segregation Principle: This principle states that clients should not be forced to depend on interfaces they do not use. Interfaces should be tailored to specific use cases.

    • Dependency Inversion Principle: This principle states that high-level modules should not depend on low-level modules. Instead, they should both depend on abstractions.

    KISS: KISS stands for "Keep it simple, stupid." This principle states that software should be designed as simply as possible, without unnecessary complexity. Simple code is easier to read, test, and maintain.

    DRY: stands for Don't Repeat Yourself. I won't repeat myself, it's self-explanatory. This applies to both one class/module that you create as for the whole application. Especially when you work with frameworks.

    Frameworks... Frameworks are also something that the cheap bootcamps mostly ommit. Basically, the whole bootcamp that you pay the $1-2k for should be an introduction to handling a framework and understanding what part of application would you like to engineer: back-end, front-end, or do you want to go for the whole stuff from the beginning.

    In addition to OOP principles and frameworks, it's also important for developers to know how to work effectively in a team. This includes skills such as documentation, task estimation, and version control. Some of the essential tools for team collaboration include:

    • Documentation: Good documentation is essential for ensuring that other team members can understand your code and use it effectively. This includes comments in the code, README files, and technical specifications.

    • Task estimation: Being able to estimate the time needed for a task is essential for effective project planning. This involves breaking down tasks into smaller sub-tasks, estimating the time required for each sub-task, and keeping track of progress.

    • Version control: Version control tools such as Git allow team members to collaborate on a codebase without overwriting each other's work. Version control also enables developers to revert to earlier versions of the code if necessary.

    Finally, it's worth noting that one year of coding on platforms such as Hyperskill by JetBrains is a great way to gain a deeper understanding of your chosen language and prepare for your first job as a developer. While bootcamps can provide a good starting point, it's important to continue learning and practicing on your own to build your skills and become a successful software developer.
Items.Author: shoshinmas

Items.Title: 9- 使用 PyQGIS 创建矢量图层和添加字段的分步指南 | 9- A Step-by-Step Guide to Creating Vector Layers and Adding Fields using PyQGIS
Items.Description:
阿扎德·拉苏尔博士

发表于 3 月 24 日

您要在 PyQGIS 中创建矢量图层和添加字段吗？ 不要再观望！ 在本分步指南中，我们将引导您完成创建新 QgsVectorLayer、添加属性表、更新字段以及向图层添加新功能的过程。 我们还将介绍如何更新图层的范围以及如何将项目列表添加到您的字段中。 无论您是经验丰富的 PyQGIS 用户还是刚刚起步，本指南都非常适合希望提高技能并将地理空间数据分析提升到新水平的任何人。 那么，让我们开始吧！

首先，创建一个新的QgsVectorLayer：

 vl = QgsVectorLayer("Point", "shapefile", "memory")

接下来，添加一个属性表：

 from qgis.PyQt.QtCore import QVariant pr = vl.dataProvider() pr.addAttributes([QgsField("City", QVariant.String), QgsField("Population", QVariant.Int), QgsField("Area", QVariant.Double)])

接下来，使用updateFields()从提供者处获取更改：

 vl.updateFields() fet = QgsFeature() fet.setGeometry(QgsGeometry.fromPointXY(QgsPointXY(44.01,36.19))) fet.setAttributes(["Erbil", 2000000, 300]) pr.addFeature(fet)

添加新功能后，我们更新层的范围

vl.updateExtents() QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(vl)

将项目列表添加到字段

vl = QgsVectorLayer("Point", "Cities_Kurdistan", "memory") from qgis.PyQt.QtCore import QVariant pr = vl.dataProvider() pr.addAttributes([QgsField("City", QVariant.String), QgsField("Population", QVariant.Double), QgsField("Area", QVariant.Double)]) vl.updateFields() QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(vl)

添加项目列表：

 f1 = QgsFeature() f1.setAttributes(['Erbil', '2932800', '14872']) f1.setGeometry(QgsGeometry.fromPointXY(QgsPointXY(44.01,36.19))) f2 = QgsFeature() f2.setAttributes(['Sulaymania', '1967000', '20143']) f2.setGeometry(QgsGeometry.fromPointXY(QgsPointXY(45.43,35.55))) f3 = QgsFeature() f3.setAttributes(['Duhok', '1292535', '10955']) f3.setGeometry(QgsGeometry.fromPointXY(QgsPointXY(42.99,36.86))) f4 = QgsFeature() f4.setAttributes(['Halabja', '109000', '889']) f4.setGeometry(QgsGeometry.fromPointXY(QgsPointXY(45.98,35.17))) vl.dataProvider().addFeatures([f1, f2, f3, f4]) vl.updateExtents()

总之，本分步指南提供了在 PyQGIS 中创建矢量图层和添加字段的全面概述。 该指南涵盖基本主题，例如创建新的 QgsVectorLayer、添加属性表、更新字段以及向图层添加新功能。 此外，该指南还介绍了如何更新图层的范围以及如何将项目列表添加到您的字段中。 无论您是经验丰富的 PyQGIS 用户还是新手，本指南都是提升您的技能并将您的地理空间数据分析提升到新水平的绝佳资源。

原文:

Dr. Azad Rasul

Posted on Mar 24

Are you looking to create vector layers and add fields in PyQGIS? Look no further! In this step-by-step guide, we will walk you through the process of creating a new QgsVectorLayer, adding an attribute table, updating fields, and adding new features to your layer. We will also cover how to update the layer's extent and add a list of items to your fields. Whether you're a seasoned PyQGIS user or just starting out, this guide is perfect for anyone looking to enhance their skills and take their geospatial data analysis to the next level. So, let's dive in!

Firstly, create a new QgsVectorLayer:

vl = QgsVectorLayer("Point", "shapefile", "memory")

Next, add an attribute table:

from qgis.PyQt.QtCore import QVariant
pr = vl.dataProvider()
pr.addAttributes([QgsField("City", QVariant.String),
                  QgsField("Population",  QVariant.Int),
                  QgsField("Area", QVariant.Double)])

Next, use updateFields() to fetch changes from the provider:

vl.updateFields()

fet = QgsFeature()
fet.setGeometry(QgsGeometry.fromPointXY(QgsPointXY(44.01,36.19)))
fet.setAttributes(["Erbil", 2000000, 300])
pr.addFeature(fet)

After new features have been added, we update layer's extent

vl.updateExtents()
QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(vl)

Add a list of items to the fields 

vl = QgsVectorLayer("Point", "Cities_Kurdistan", "memory")

from qgis.PyQt.QtCore import QVariant
pr = vl.dataProvider()
pr.addAttributes([QgsField("City", QVariant.String),
                  QgsField("Population",  QVariant.Double),
                  QgsField("Area", QVariant.Double)])
vl.updateFields()

QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(vl)

Add a list of items:

f1 = QgsFeature()
f1.setAttributes(['Erbil', '2932800', '14872'])
f1.setGeometry(QgsGeometry.fromPointXY(QgsPointXY(44.01,36.19)))

f2 = QgsFeature()
f2.setAttributes(['Sulaymania', '1967000', '20143'])
f2.setGeometry(QgsGeometry.fromPointXY(QgsPointXY(45.43,35.55)))

f3 = QgsFeature()
f3.setAttributes(['Duhok', '1292535', '10955'])
f3.setGeometry(QgsGeometry.fromPointXY(QgsPointXY(42.99,36.86)))

f4 = QgsFeature()
f4.setAttributes(['Halabja', '109000', '889'])
f4.setGeometry(QgsGeometry.fromPointXY(QgsPointXY(45.98,35.17)))

vl.dataProvider().addFeatures([f1, f2, f3, f4])

vl.updateExtents()

In conclusion, this step-by-step guide provides a comprehensive overview of creating vector layers and adding fields in PyQGIS. The guide covers essential topics such as creating a new QgsVectorLayer, adding an attribute table, updating fields, and adding new features to your layer. Additionally, the guide also covers how to update the layer's extent and add a list of items to your fields. Whether you're an experienced PyQGIS user or just starting, this guide is an excellent resource to enhance your skills and take your geospatial data analysis to the next level.

Items.Title: 8- 如何确定栅格图层在 PyQGIS 中是否有效 | 8- How to Determine if a Raster Layer is Valid in PyQGIS
Items.Description:
阿扎德·拉苏尔博士

发表于 3 月 24 日

您是 PyQGIS 用户，正在努力验证栅格图层吗？ 不要再观望！ 在本文中，我们将指导您完成使用 PyQGIS 检查栅格图层有效性的过程。 通过我们的分步说明，您将能够轻松确定栅格图层是否有效。 因此，让我们开始吧，确保您的栅格图层已准备好在您的 PyQGIS 项目中使用。

加载栅格图层：

 uri = "D:/Python_QGIS/data/dem_subset.tif" rlayer = iface.addRasterLayer(uri, "my_raster", "gdal")

检查栅格图层是否有效：

 if rlayer.isValid(): print("Great this is a valid raster layer!") else: print("This is invalid raster layer!")

如果图层有效，您就可以开始了！ 如果图层无效，则图层的元数据、投影或数据格式可能存在问题。 您可以尝试以不同的格式重新保存图层或检查元数据以查看是否有任何错误。

总之，检查栅格图层的有效性是确保它可以在 PyQGIS 项目中有效使用的重要步骤。 按照本文中概述的步骤，PyQGIS 用户可以轻松确定其栅格图层是否有效。 此外，如果图层无效，用户可以采取措施排除故障并解决任何问题。 请记住在项目中使用栅格图层之前始终验证它们！

Posted on Mar 24

Are you a PyQGIS user struggling with validating raster layers? Look no further! In this article, we will guide you through the process of checking the validity of a raster layer using PyQGIS. With our step-by-step instructions, you'll be able to easily determine whether your raster layer is valid or not. So, let's get started and ensure that your raster layer is ready to be used in your PyQGIS project.

Load a raster layer:

uri = "D:/Python_QGIS/data/dem_subset.tif"
rlayer = iface.addRasterLayer(uri, "my_raster", "gdal")

Check if the raster layer is valid or not:

if rlayer.isValid():
    print("Great this is a valid raster layer!")
else: 
    print("This is invalid raster layer!")

If the layer is valid, you're good to go! If the layer is not valid, there may be an issue with the layer's metadata, projection, or data format. You can try re-saving the layer in a different format or checking the metadata to see if there are any errors.

In conclusion, checking the validity of a raster layer is an important step in ensuring that it can be used effectively in a PyQGIS project. By following the steps outlined in this article, PyQGIS users can easily determine whether their raster layer is valid or not. Additionally, if the layer is not valid, users can take steps to troubleshoot and resolve any issues. Remember to always validate your raster layers before using them in a project!
